dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: My lords, please turn aside to your servant's house and spend the night and wash your feet; then you may rise early and go on your way

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: No, we will spend the night in the town square

dialogue.speaker: men-of-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Where are the men who came to you tonight? Bring them out to us, that we may know them

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: I beg you, my brothers, do not act so wickedly

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have two daughters who have not known any man; let me bring them out to you, and do to them as you please; only do nothing to these men, for they have come under the shelter of my roof

dialogue.speaker: men-of-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Stand back!

dialogue.speaker: men-of-sodom
dialogue.englishText: This fellow came to sojourn, and he would play the judge! Now we will deal worse with you than with them

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Have you anyone else here? Son-in-law, sons, daughters, or anyone you have in the city — bring them out of the place

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: For we are about to destroy this place, because the outcry against its people has become great before YHWH, and YHWH has sent us to destroy it

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: Up, get out of this place, for YHWH is about to destroy the city

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Up! Take your wife and your two daughters who are here, lest you be swept away in the punishment of the city

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Escape for your life; do not look back or stop anywhere in the valley; escape to the hills, lest you be swept away

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: Oh no, my lords

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: Behold, your servant has found favor in your sight, and you have shown me great kindness in saving my life; but I cannot escape to the hills, lest the disaster overtake me and I die

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: Behold, this city is near enough to flee to, and it is a little one; let me escape there — is it not a little one? — and my life will be saved

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I grant you this favor also, that I will not overthrow the city of which you have spoken

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Escape there quickly, for I can do nothing until you arrive there

dialogue.speaker: lots-daughters
dialogue.englishText: Our father is old, and there is not a man on earth to come in to us after the manner of all the earth

dialogue.speaker: lots-daughters
dialogue.englishText: Come, let us make our father drink wine and we will lie with him, that we may preserve offspring from our father

dialogue.speaker: lots-daughters
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I lay last night with my father; let us make him drink wine tonight also, and you go in and lie with him, that we may preserve offspring from our father
